---
title: "GEA analysis"
output:
  github_document:
    toc: TRUE
---
```{r, include = FALSE}
library(here)
library(tidyverse)
library(sf)
library(terra)
library(ggpubr)
library(algatr)
library(viridis)
library(vcfR)
library(hierfstat)
library(tigris)

devtools::load_all()
source(here("analysis", "gea", "gea.R"))
source(here("general_functions.R"))

# Set global options for caching
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  cache = TRUE,
  echo = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  result = FALSE
)
```

```{r, echo = FALSE, message = FALSE, include=FALSE}
# Load the U.S. state boundaries data
ca <- get_ca()

# make theme for plotting
theme_custom <- 
  theme(
    strip.background = element_rect(color = "gray", fill = "gray"),
    panel.background = element_rect(color = "gray", fill = NA),
    axis.text = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 16, face = "italic")
    )
```

```{r}
# get coordinates 
coords <- get_coords(sf = TRUE)
```

```{r}
# get vcf 
vcf <- read.vcfR(here("58-Sceloporus", "58-Sceloporus_JALMGF010000001.1.vcf.gz"))
dos <- vcf_to_dosage(vcf)
```

```{r}
coords <-
  coords %>%
  filter(SampleID %in% colnames(vcf@gt)[-1]) %>%
  mutate(SampleID = factor(SampleID, levels = colnames(vcf@gt)[-1])) %>%
  arrange(SampleID)

stopifnot(all(coords$SampleID == colnames(vcf@gt)[-1]))
```
```{r}
dem <- elevatr::get_elev_raster(coords, z = 10)
coords$elevation <- extract(dem, coords)
```
```{r}
# deal with NA values (for now just removing SNPs with NA values)
#dos <- simple_impute(dos)
na <- apply(dos, 2, function(x) mean(is.na(x)))
doscc <- dos[,(na == 0)]

# extract env data and only retain complete cases
env <- cbind(data.frame(dem = extract(dem, coords)), extract(CA_env, coords))
cci <- complete.cases(env)
env_cci <- env[cci,]

# run RDA
coords_mat <- st_coordinates(coords)[cci,]
colnames(coords_mat) <- c("x", "y")
mod_full <- rda_run(doscc[cci,], env_cci, coords = coords_mat, model = "full", correctGEO = TRUE)

# get outliers
# note: plotting will cause a crash because there are so many snps
rda_sig_p <- rda_getoutliers(mod_full, naxes = "all", outlier_method = "p", p_adj = "fdr", sig = 0.05, plot = FALSE)

# pull out outliers from dosage matrix 
rda_snps <- rda_sig_p$rda_snps
rda_gen <- doscc[cci, rda_snps]

# test for correlations
cor_df <- rda_cor(rda_gen, cbind(env[cci,], coords_mat))
cor_df <- 
  cor_df %>%
  filter(p < 0.05) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(snp) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(strongest = (abs(r) == max(abs(r))))

cor_df %>% group_by(var) %>% summarize(count = n())
cor_df %>% arrange(desc(abs(r)))

write.csv(rda_gen, here("analysis", "gea", "outputs", "rda_gen.csv"), row.names = TRUE)
write.csv(rda_snps, here("analysis", "gea", "outputs", "rda_snps.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(cor_df, here("analysis", "gea", "outputs", "cor_df.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

#arrange by loading
rda_sig_p %>% arrange(loading) %>% head()
```

```{r}

cor_df <- 
  read.csv(here("analysis", "gea", "outputs", "cor_df.csv"))

geo_snps <- 
  cor_df %>%
  filter((var == "y" & strongest) | (var == "y" & r > 0.4)) %>%
  pull(snp)

top_snps <- 
  cor_df %>%
  filter(!(snp %in% geo_snps)) %>%
  arrange(desc(abs(r))) %>%
  head() %>%
  pull(snp)

rda_gen <- 
  read.csv(here("analysis", "gea", "outputs", "rda_gen.csv")) %>%
  data.frame() %>%
  rename(SampleID = X)

rda_top5 <- rda_gen[,c("SampleID", top_snps)]

rda_df <-
  left_join(coords, rda_top5) %>%
  pivot_longer(c(-SampleID, -geometry))

ggplot(rda_df) +
  geom_sf(data = ca) +
  geom_sf(aes(col = value)) +
  facet_wrap(~name) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "viridis", end = 0.9) +
  theme_void()
```

```{r}
library(MASS)

df <- 
  left_join(st_drop_geometry(coords), rda_top5) %>%
  bind_cols(st_coordinates(coords)) %>%
  mutate(class = (JALMGF010000001.1_120374359 != 0))

model <- lda(class ~ X + Y, data = df)
predictions <- predict(model, df)$class
observed <- df$class
error <- sum(predictions != observed, na.rm = TRUE) / length(na.omit(observed))

df <- df %>% mutate(class_factor = factor(class))
klaR::partimat(class_factor ~ X + Y, data = df, method = "lda")

```

```{r}
df <- 
  left_join(st_drop_geometry(coords), rda_gen) %>%
  bind_cols(st_coordinates(coords)) %>%
  mutate(across(starts_with("JALMGF010000001"), ~(.x != 0)))

snps <- colnames(dplyr::select(df, starts_with("JALMGF010000001")))
errors <- 
  map_dbl(snps, ~{ 
    f <- formula(paste0(.x, "~ X + Y"))
    model <- lda(f, data = df)
    predictions <- predict(model, df)$class
    observed <- pull(df,.x)
    error <- sum(predictions != observed, na.rm = TRUE) / length(na.omit(observed))
    return(error)
  })

errors_df <- 
  data.frame(error = errors, snp = snps) %>%
  left_join(cor_df) %>%
  filter(var == "y")

plot(abs(errors_df$r), errors_df$error)

high_snps <- errors_df %>% arrange(desc(error)) %>% head(9) %>% pull(snp)
low_snps <- errors_df %>% arrange(error) %>% head(9) %>% pull(snp)

rda_top <- rda_gen[,c("SampleID", high_snps)]
rda_bottom <- rda_gen[,c("SampleID", low_snps)]

rda_top_df <-
  left_join(coords, rda_top) %>%
  pivot_longer(c(-SampleID, -geometry))
  
rda_bottom_df <-
  left_join(coords, rda_bottom) %>%
  pivot_longer(c(-SampleID, -geometry))

ggplot(rda_top_df) +
  geom_sf(data = ca) +
  geom_sf(aes(col = value)) +
  facet_wrap(~name) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "viridis", end = 0.9) +
  theme_void()


ggplot(rda_bottom_df) +
  geom_sf(data = ca) +
  geom_sf(aes(col = value)) +
  facet_wrap(~name) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "viridis", end = 0.9) +
  theme_void()

# TRY RF BUT SET THE NUMBER OF TREE BRANCHES AND SEE HOW ERROR CHANGES
# See how it drops off from 2 to 3 to 4
# measure of dataset replication
```

```{r}
library(randomForest)
df <- 
  left_join(st_drop_geometry(coords), rda_top5) %>%
  bind_cols(st_coordinates(coords)) %>%
  mutate(class = (JALMGF010000001.1_120374359 != 0))


# Set maxnodes to 2 to limit each tree to one split
rf_simple <- randomForest(class ~ X + Y, data = drop_na(df, class, X, Y), maxnodes = 2)


library(rpart)

df <- 
  left_join(st_drop_geometry(coords), rda_gen) %>%
  bind_cols(st_coordinates(coords)) %>%
  mutate(across(starts_with("JALMGF010000001"), ~(.x != 0)))

snps <- colnames(dplyr::select(df, starts_with("JALMGF010000001")))
errors_1split <- 
  map_dbl(snps, ~{ 
    f <- formula(paste0(.x, "~ X + Y"))
    # Building a simple decision tree model with one split
    tree_model <- rpart(f, data = df, method="class", control=rpart.control(maxdepth = 1))

    # Making predictions on the testing set
    predictions <- predict(tree_model, newdata = df, type = "class")

    # Calculating classification error
    error <- mean(predictions != pull(df, .x), na.rm = TRUE)
    return(error)
  }) 

errors_2split <- 
  map_dbl(snps, ~{ 
    f <- formula(paste0(.x, "~ X + Y"))
    # Building a simple decision tree model with two splits
    tree_model <- rpart(f, data = df, method="class", control=rpart.control(maxdepth = 2))

    # Making predictions on the testing set
    predictions <- predict(tree_model, newdata = df, type = "class")

    # Calculating classification error
    error <- mean(predictions != pull(df, .x), na.rm = TRUE)
    return(error)
  }) 

error_df <- 
  data.frame(error1 = errors_1split, error2 = errors_2split, snp = snps) %>%
  left_join(cor_df) %>%
  filter(var == "y")

plot(error_df$error1, abs(error_df$r))
plot(error_df$error2, abs(error_df$r))


snp_df <-
  left_join(coords, rda_gen) %>%
  pivot_longer(c(-SampleID, -geometry), names_to = "snp")

error1_snps_max <- error_df %>% arrange(desc(error1)) %>% head(4) %>% mutate(group = "Error 1 Max") %>% left_join(snp_df)
error2_snps_max <- error_df %>% arrange(desc(error2)) %>% head(4) %>% mutate(group = "Error 2 Max") %>% left_join(snp_df)
error1_snps_min <- error_df %>% arrange((error1)) %>% head(4) %>% mutate(group = "Error 1 Min") %>% left_join(snp_df)
error2_snps_min <- error_df %>% arrange((error2)) %>% head(4) %>% mutate(group = "Error 2 Min") %>% left_join(snp_df)
error_min_max <- bind_rows(error1_snps_max, error2_snps_max, error1_snps_min, error2_snps_min)

ggplot(error_min_max) +
  geom_sf(data = ca) +
  geom_sf(aes(col = value, geometry = geometry)) +
  facet_wrap(~group+snp) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "viridis", end = 0.9) +
  theme_void()


```
